name: Hypericin
description: Hypericin is under investigation for the treatment of Cutaneous T-cell Lymphoma.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The risk or severity of QTc prolongation can be increased when Vemurafenib is combined with Hypericin.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Hypericin is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Hypericin.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Hypericin may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Hypericin.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Hypericin.

interactions.name: Rabies virus inactivated antigen, A
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Hypericin is combined with Rabies virus inactivated antigen, A.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Hypericin.

interactions.name: Leflunomide
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Hypericin is combined with Leflunomide.

interactions.name: Fingolimod
interactions.description: Hypericin may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Fingolimod.

interactions.name: Roflumilast
interactions.description: Roflumilast may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Hypericin.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with Hypericin.

interactions.name: Varicella Zoster Vaccine (Live/Attenuated)
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Varicella Zoster Vaccine (Live/Attenuated) can be decreased when used in combination with Hypericin.
